generated_for_sub_pass: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.4 — Auth Callback and Session Guard Precondition

callback_session_behavior.callback_exchange_scope: exchangeCodeForSession may be called only when public runtime config exists, the conditional client initializes, a code parameter is present, and the function is explicitly invoked.
callback_session_behavior.session_guard_scope: getSession may be called only to classify session state and never to enter a protected shell.

claimable_after_this_pass: A bounded Supabase auth callback/session-guard precondition exists; the repo can classify provider-return/session state when externally supplied public config and callback input exist.
not_claimable_after_this_pass: user can initiate provider login from the product

next_threshold.name: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.5 — Login Initiation or Protected Shell Authorization Boundary
next_threshold.purpose: Separately authorize either a real login initiation surface or a protected shell boundary without conflating callback/session classification with Authenticated Surfaces birth.

hard_boundaries_preserved: No login UI, signup UI, provider-login initiation, credential capture, protected route entry, or protected shell entry is implemented.
No callback HTML route is created in this pass.
No backend, database schema, database access, persistence layer, RLS, tenant isolation, customer workspace, billing, Palisade, Weave runtime, or public NEXUS runtime is implemented.
No Supabase runtime public config values or package files are committed.
Server-secret names remain bounded to validators and data records as forbidden terms.
